title: ACER: port ptlegc/coul (LTP=1/2 Legendre charged-particle elastic)
description: acecpe_one_incident (ace_charged.jl:93) only handles LTP=12 (T50). LTP=1/2 (Legendre nuclear-amplitude / residual-XS expansion) needs ptlegc+coul (acefc.f90:7980-8201) adaptive Legendre->tabulated reconstruction before the acecpe loop. Call from acecpe when sub.ltp<12. RED BAR: T52 (p+H1, LTP=1 LIDP=1 s=0.5, NTR=0/NR=0/NTRP=0/NTYPE=0 — only MT=2 elastic, cleanest possible test).
close_reason: Ported ptlegc/coul (acefc.f90:7980-8201) for LTP=1/2 charged-particle elastic. T52 tape34 BIT_IDENTICAL 3986/3986 (verified). T50 regression-clean 163/163. Root cause of under-refinement was nt=sub.nl (LIST CONT N2, not a data value).

title: errorr/rescon: T20 CRASH on read_mf32 LRF=7 (regression) — graceful-skip interim
description: Fresh sweep: T20 (Cl-35 MAT=1725 SAMMY/RML) CRASHes: 'read_mf32: range 1 LRF=7 not yet supported'. REGRESSION introduced by Phase 72-76 rescon (read_mf32/apply_rescon!); T20 was DIFFS pre-rescon. Fortran handles LRF=7 MF32 via rpxsamm (errorr.f90:3252). INTERIM FIX: read_mf32/apply_rescon! should gracefully SKIP LRF=4 (Adler) and LRF=7 (RML) MF32 ranges with a @warn instead of error(), so errorr still produces T20's tapes. Files: src/processing/mf32_reader.jl (the LRF error site), src/processing/rescon.jl (apply_rescon! must tolerate skipped ranges). Proper port = NJOY_jl rpxsamm bead.
acceptance_criteria: T20 CRASH->DIFFS (produces tape23+tape27); T15/T16/T17 (supported LRF=3+URR) unchanged byte-for-byte; no other regression.
close_reason: Fixed: read_mf32 unported sub-formats (LRF=4/7, LCOMP!=1, NRO!=0, NLRS!=0, ISR=1+LRF=3, LRU!=1) now @warn+skip instead of error(). T20 CRASH->DIFFS (tape23 28549, tape27 30111). Supported LRF=3+URR byte-unchanged (mf32_reader 83/83, covcal_lb5 50/50, tape26=5958, canary OK). Crashes 0/84. Proper rpxsamm port = NJOY_jl-4pz.

title: ACER: tighten T50 tape34 1e-5 -> 1e-9 (acecpe FP grind)
description: 60 sub-1e-5 lines remain in T50 tape34: FP in per-mu Coulomb eval, trapezoidal cumm accumulation order, log-log interp on yys. Fortran acecpe (acefc.f90 ~6492-6671): write(*,...) sigc/signi/cumm/signow, match Julia order. Files: src/formats/ace_charged.jl, ace_builder.jl.
acceptance_criteria: T50 tape34 163/163 bit-identical @1e-9; no regression on T01 ACE.
notes: Corrected state: T50 is 143/163 bit-identical (not 103). 20 lines fail @1e-9 AND @1e-7 but pass @1e-5; magnitudes ~1e-6 = REAL computation-path difference, NOT ULP noise. Likely off-by-one in acecpe adaptive subdivision near mu=1 (ace_charged.jl:121-196 vs acefc.f90:6566-6624). Fortran already prints per-mu sigc/cumm at acefc.f90:6607-6608 — diff vs Julia per-mu dump. ~half-session focused FP grind.
close_reason: T50 tape34 143/163 -> 163/163 BIT_IDENTICAL @1e-9. Root cause: acecpe reads sigfig-rounded ESZ columns (xelas/signi from sigfig7 elastic; total=sigfig9(Σ sigfig7)); _log_log_interp rewritten to mirror terp1 int=5. No regression (T01 32812/32962, T02 12519/13873). Independently re-verified 163/163.

title: ACER promotion: T50 @1e-9 + 8 sibling tests
description: Promote acer_module to a real ACE generator. T50 at NUMERIC_PASS 1e-5 (143/163 bit-identical). Unlocks T14,T48,T51-54,T62,T71. HANDOFF P1 ACER; worklog/T50_acer_phase1_scaffolding.md.
notes:
Research 2026-05-28: 'unlocks 8 siblings' is OVERSTATED. T51-54/T62/T71 are NOT free riders — each has a 3-25x XSS shortfall (missing MF6 multi-MT reaction processing; T52 needs LTP<12 Legendre->tabular conv). T48 is photoatomic iopt=4 (unrelated path). T14 has a ZAID-suffix parser issue + structural shortfall. Closing T50 to 1e-9 unlocks ONLY T50; each sibling is a separate substantial effort.
T50 (cnh.1) DONE — bit-identical. Remaining children: cnh.2 (siblings T51-54/T62/T71 — each needs separate MF6 multi-MT / LTP<12 work, NOT free riders), cnh.3 (T14 suffix+structural, T48 photoatomic). Epic stays open for those.

title: broadr: optimize hot loop (prealloc/devectorize), target <300s
description: Devectorize/preallocate the broadr hot loop per profiling. Preserve exact FP semantics (no numeric regression). Files: src/processing/broadr.jl, sigma1.jl.
acceptance_criteria: T15 broadr completes <300s, tapes 22/23 produced, broadened XS bit-identical to pre-optimization output.
close_reason: DONE. Velocity-hoist landed + verified: U-238 (T15) broadr now 40s (was ~1039s), ~26x, byte-identical (T08 tape23 md5 unchanged; T01 tape25 32812/32962). <300s target met.

title: broadr: profile sigma1 Doppler hot loop (U-238 URR)
description: @profile / flamegraph broadn_grid + sigma1_at on the U-238 (T15) case. Identify the hot loop. No code change -- diagnosis only. Files: src/processing/broadr.jl, src/processing/sigma1.jl.
acceptance_criteria: Flamegraph captured; named hotspot(s) identified with line numbers.
close_reason: ROOT CAUSE: sigma1.jl:146 'v = map(e->sqrt(alpha*e), seg_e)' rebuilds + heap-allocs the full length-nseg velocity array on EVERY sigma1_at call. broadn_grid calls sigma1_at O(nseg) times -> O(nseg^2) time + O(nseg^2) allocs (20k-pt synthetic: 1.79s / 1.82 GB). Fortran converts E->v ONCE per page (broadr.f90:1318-1322); broadn_grid already computes vel=sqrt.(alpha.*seg_e) at line 79 but sigma1_at ignores it. FP-safe: map/broadcast/scalar sqrt all bit-identical (verified). Fix in 58o.2: thread precomputed vel through sigma1_at.

title: broadr U-238 JENDL performance (T15/T17 TIMEOUT)
description: T15/T17 full-pipeline timeout in broadr (~1039s vs Fortran ~30s) over U-238 dense URR tabulation. Blocks end-to-end validation of all Phase 71-76 T15 covcal/rescon work. HANDOFF P2 broadr perf (retired bead NJOY.jl-326). Files: src/processing/broadr.jl, sigma1.jl.
close_reason: broadr U-238 perf SATISFIED: 40s << 300s. T15/T17 broadr no longer the bottleneck; unblocks end-to-end validation of Phase 71-76 covcal/rescon work (=> NJOY_jl-zso).

title: leapr contin Phase B: naint SCT-gating (T80 toward bit-identical)
description: Fortran contin (leapr.f90:584-642) wraps ssm(k,j)=ssct inside iprt=mod(j-1,naint)+1==1 -- only every naint-th alpha (+ j=nalpha) gets SCT replacement; Julia replaces all unconditionally. Thread LeaprParams.naint into generate_sab. Files: src/processing/leapr.jl, src/orchestration/modules/leapr.jl. HANDOFF P2 leapr Phase B; worklog/T80_leapr_contin_phase_a_lat_sc.md.
acceptance_criteria: T80 tape24 closer to 91453/91453 @1e-9; T22 stays BIT_IDENTICAL 4636/4636.
close_reason: Not a bug — misdiagnosis from stale HANDOFF. naint is hardcoded to 1 in Fortran (leapr.f90:292-294, never read from input), so the iprt=mod(j-1,naint)+1==1 gate is always open and Julia's unconditional SCT replacement already matches Fortran. Corrected the leapr.jl comment + HANDOFF. Real T80 residual is phonon-loop FP order = NJOY_jl-sc5.

title: P0: Re-run full 84-test reference sweep (post-Phase-76 baseline)
description: REFERENCE_SWEEP.md is stale (Phase 67, 2026-05-03), predates Phases 68-76. Re-run test/validation/sweep_reference_tests.jl for the true current baseline. Nuke precomp cache first. ~90 min, background. Cannot run concurrently with any other Julia (Rule 9).
acceptance_criteria: reports/REFERENCE_SWEEP.md regenerated reflecting post-Phase-76 state; crash count and DIFFS baseline current.
close_reason: Fresh sweep landed (80 min): 2 BIT_IDENTICAL, 2 NUMERIC_PASS, 78 DIFFS, 1 CRASH (T20 regression), 1 NO_REFERENCE. Crashes 4->1 (broadr fix let T11/T15/T16/T65 complete). reports/REFERENCE_SWEEP.md + HANDOFF Phase 77 updated. Unblocks NJOY_jl-tbe (DIFFS grind).

title: ACER: T62 (d+He3) tape34 61-line value grind -> BIT_IDENTICAL
description: Post-ptlegc T62 tape34 DIFFS 7160/7221, line count exact (7221=7221). 61 value-differing lines. T62 is d+He3 = non-identical particles (LIDP=0), so this likely exercises the coul LIDP=0 Coulomb branch that T52 (LIDP=1, BIT_IDENTICAL) did not. Grind-Method: first differing line on tape34 -> root cause -> match Fortran FP. Fortran coul/ptlegc/acecpe (acefc.f90:7980-8201, acecpe ~6529-6589). Regression guards: T52 3986/3986, T50 163/163.
close_reason: T62 tape34 BIT_IDENTICAL 7221/7221 (verified). Root causes (all deep, all cited): (1) MF3 interp law dropped for charged INT=5/6 reactions (MT=600 INT=6 Coulomb penetrability) -> new _parse_mf3_tab1/extract_mf3_tab1_all carry NBT/INT+QM/QI, acer samples with native law (terp1 i=6, endf.f90:1634); (2) disappear column = sum of sigfig-7 reaction xs mt<=200||mt>=600 (acelod acefc.f90:5648), not max(total-elastic,0); (3) heating zeroed for izai<=2004 (acefc.f90:6663); (4) LQR = QI/1e6 MeV; (5) a11 e20.11 drops E for |exp|>=100 (acecm.f90:791). Regression-clean: T52 3986/3986, T50 163/163, T02 tape28 12519/13873 NUMERIC_PASS unchanged, T08 unchanged.

title: thermr: T70 lat=10 coherent-elastic MF3 grid over-production (213800 vs 112817)
description: lat=10 read path (read_mf7_mt2) landed; T70 tape60 now emits the coherent-elastic section but the MF3 energy grid is ~1.9x over-produced (Julia 213800 lines vs ref 112817), only 1 line matches. Likely build_thermal_grid over-refines the 568 Al-27 Bragg edges, or the lat=10 BraggData (scon=1, no ulim sentinel, n_edges=NP) feeds the adaptive grid differently than Fortran coh. Diagnose by tallying MF3 per-MT line counts Julia vs ref (re-run needed; T70 is ~206s/iter). Fortran: coh adaptive merge (thermr.f90 ~429+) + sigcoh lat=10 (1150-1166). NOTE T70 also has non-elastic (inelastic S(a,b)) diffs per scoping — full NUMERIC_PASS needs both.

title: thermr: wire incoherent-elastic (iel, LTHR=2) into pipeline
description: incoh_elastic_xs() exists in thermr.jl but is never called. Fortran coh dispatch sets icoh=10*lthr; LTHR=2 -> icoh=20 -> iel(20,...) (thermr.f90:428-434, iel ~1288-1424). Wire it for LTHR=2 (and the iel half of LTHR=3). Unblocks the elastic section of T69 (Zr-in-ZrH), T74 (H-in-ZrH), and the iel half of T67 (D-in-LiD). Acceptance: those tests gain a real incoherent-elastic section vs ref.

title: ACER: secondary-neutron DLW energy distributions (NR>0)
description: Port DLW block for secondary-neutron-producing charged-particle reactions (currently sentinel only). Depends on photon-prod. Acceptance: T51 NUMERIC_PASS; T14 -> DIFFS w/ meaningful ratio.

title: ACER: photon-production + particle-type blocks (NTRP>0/NTYPE>0)
description: Port GPD/MTRP/LSIGP/SIGP/LANDP/ANDP/LDLWP/DLWP + PTYPE/NTRO/PLOCT for charged-particle iopt=1. Depends on ptlegc. Acceptance: T53,T54 NUMERIC_PASS.
notes: Phase finding (from 4ee): T54 (LTP=1 LIDP=0, different particles) underproduces tape34 (1347 vs 7327) — needs photon-prod/particle-type blocks AND possibly its own lidp=0 elastic path check. T53(LIDP=1 spin=1)/T62 elastic MT=2 reconstruction verified correct; their gaps are NTRP photon-prod + DLW.

description: input_parser.jl:308 default suffix '80c' should be '00c' (Fortran suff=0 -> zaid .00). Acceptance: T14 tape31 line1 ZAID 7014.00h. One-liner.

title: thermr: read MF7/MT2 Bragg lattice from ENDF (remove hardcoded table)
description: Replace hardcoded BRAGG_LATTICE_PARAMS with per-material MF7/MT2 reads. Unblocks T25,T67-70,T74 (6-7 thermal tests on stubs). Fortran thermr.f90 bragg. Files: src/processing/thermr.jl, src/orchestration/auto_params.jl.
acceptance_criteria: BRAGG_LATTICE_PARAMS removed; T25,T67,T68,T69,T70,T74 use real MF7/MT2 reads.
notes: PREMISE CORRECTED (verified vs Fortran 2026-05-28): do NOT 'replace the hardcoded table'. Fortran sigcoh (thermr.f90:947-1207) supports BOTH: lat=1/2/3 = hardcoded graphite/Be/BeO (NJOY.jl BRAGG_LATTICE_PARAMS is FAITHFUL to these — keep them), and lat=10 = read reciprocal-lattice/Bragg data from ENDF-6 MF7/MT2 ('if(lat.eq.10) go to 200', thermr.f90:1016; coh caller picks coh(10,..) when MF7/MT2 present, thermr.f90:429/433). Correct scope: ADD the lat=10 MF7/MT2 coherent-elastic read path (port sigcoh's go-to-200 branch + the coh dispatch), KEEP the built-in lattices. T25/T67-70/T74 likely need lat=10. Re-verify which lat each of those tests uses before coding.
close_reason: lat=10 coherent-elastic MF7/MT2 read path implemented (read_mf7_mt2, sigcoh lat=10 transform, coh LTHR dispatch). Kept hardcoded lat=1/2/3 fallback. Also fixed latent read_mf7_mt4 bug: natom was read from B(4) (=emax 2.277->2 for Al, a /2 bound-XS error) instead of the input card (thermr.f90:1670-1676). T01 regression-clean (32812/32962, byte-identical -> natom no-op for H-in-H2O). Output-grid fidelity for T70 tracked in new grid-over-production bead; iel/LTHR=2 in NJOY_jl-69j.

title: CCCCR: wire GENDF -> ISOTXS/BRKOXS/DLAYXS (module is a stub)
description: MISSING. The CCCC binary writers exist (src/formats/ccccr.jl, ccccr_b.jl: write_isotxs/brkoxs/dlayxs) but src/orchestration/modules/ccccr.jl is a marker-text stub — it never parses the GENDF input or feeds the writers ('GENDF format is complex; full parsing not yet implemented'). Sweep fingerprint: T56-59/T64/T66/T78 emit 0-line tapes (tape31/34/37 0/0). Fortran: njoy-reference/src/ccccr.f90 (cisotx/cbrkxs/cdlyxs + its GENDF reader).
acceptance_criteria: UNBOUNDED — true scope is unknown until the Fortran is read. FIRST work step when picked up: a scoping pass (read the cited .f90, enumerate sub-features + which reference tests need each) that FILES ONE CHILD BEAD per sub-feature/test. This epic closes only when its children close. Do NOT attempt a monolithic implementation. All ccccr reference tests (T56-59/T64/T66/T78) produce non-empty ISOTXS/BRKOXS/DLAYXS matching reference.

title: errorr: port rpxsamm for LRF=7/LRF=4 MF32 resonance-param cov (proper T20 fix)
description: Proper fix for T20 (and other SAMMY/RML + Adler evaluations): port Fortran rpxsamm (errorr.f90:3252+) so read_mf32/rescon compute MF32->MF33 sandwich contributions for LRF=7 (R-Matrix Limited) and LRF=4 (Adler-Adler), not just LRF=1/2/3 (BW/RM) + URR. Depends on the graceful-skip interim landing first.
acceptance_criteria: T20 covariance includes LRF=7 rescon contributions matching reference.

title: broadr: sigma1.jl:67 ntuple(5)->ntuple(Val(5)) (eliminate per-interval closure alloc)
description: h_all's h=ntuple(5) do k (sigma1.jl:67) heap-allocs a closure per interval (~239 B/call, O(nseg^2) total over a broadr run). ntuple(f,Val(5)) forces compile-time unroll, eliminating it. Verified bit-identical over 20k tuples (max diff 0.0); matches codebase convention (Val(62) faddeeva_table.jl, Val(50) sigma1.jl). Not needed for <300s (already met) but a free GC win. Optionally also h_taylor MVector{50} (rare path).
acceptance_criteria: sigma1.jl:67 uses Val(5); T01 tape25 stays 32812/32962 byte-identical; broadr GC pressure reduced.
close_reason: Landed: sigma1.jl:67 ntuple(5)->ntuple(Val(5)). Byte-identical (T01 tape25 32812/32962). Eliminates per-interval closure alloc.

title: Grind remaining DIFFS per-tape (Grind Method, after fresh sweep)
description: Per-tape bisection via the Grind Method on remaining DIFFS once the post-Phase-76 sweep baseline exists. Each tape: diff cols 1-66, classify, read Fortran, fix.
acceptance_criteria: Each targeted DIFFS tape -> NUMERIC_PASS or better.
